name: Asymmetric dark matter
summary: Dark matter carries a conserved 'dark baryon number' analogous to baryon number. A primordial asymmetry in the dark sector, related to the visible baryon asymmetry, sets the relic abundance via annihilation of the symmetric component leaving only the asymmetric residue. Naturally explains the cosmic coincidence Omega_DM / Omega_b ~ 5: if asymmetries are similar, m_DM / m_proton ~ 5.

addresses_puzzles.note: Predicts m_DM ~ few GeV (compatible with sub-GeV direct-detection sensitivity) when DM and baryon asymmetries are comparable. Specific models tie the dark asymmetry to leptogenesis or Affleck-Dine.
Connects the BAU to DM origin via a shared mechanism producing both asymmetries; addresses 'why DM and baryons are within a factor of 5' as a structural prediction rather than a coincidence.

touches_benchmarks.note: ADM is typically lighter (few GeV) than canonical WIMPs; direct-detection bounds in this mass window are weaker but rapidly improving (CRESST, SuperCDMS, light-WIMP analyses).

introduces.note: DM mass, asymmetry, sharing mechanism with visible sector, symmetric-component annihilation cross-section.

history: Nussinov (1985) for DM ~ pi-related mass; Kaplan-Luty-Zurek (2009) modern treatment. Active in the past decade as direct-detection bounds push canonical WIMP scenarios.

references.citation: Kaplan, Luty, Zurek, 'Asymmetric dark matter', Phys. Rev. D 79, 115016 (2009)
Petraki and Volkas, 'Review of asymmetric dark matter', Int. J. Mod. Phys. A 28, 1330028 (2013)
